steps.title: Construct the confidence interval
steps.content: Estimate = 0.49, margin of error = 0.04. Plausible range: [0.49 - 0.04, 0.49 + 0.04] = [0.45, 0.53].

steps.title: Interpret what the interval means
steps.content: Any proportion within [0.45, 0.53] is plausible. Proportions outside this range are not supported by the sample data.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It is plausible that the proportion is between 0.45 and 0.53.': This exactly describes the confidence interval [0.45, 0.53]. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It is plausible that the proportion is less than 0.45.': Values below 0.45 are outside the interval; the margin of error does not support this. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The proportion is exactly 0.49.': 0.49 is the point estimate, not the true population proportion; we cannot claim exactness. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It is plausible that the proportion is greater than 0.53.': Values above 0.53 are outside the interval; not supported. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
